## Recommended Actions

1. **Start N8N** — Check if N8N is installed and start it (`n8n start`, `docker start n8n`, or via PM2/systemd, depending on your setup).
2. **Verify it is up** — `curl http://localhost:5678/healthz` should return `{"status":"ok"}`.
3. **Save API Key** — Save your N8N API key to `~/Documents/ThurrSolutions/Thurnos/memory/n8n_api_key.txt` (plain text, no quotes or extra whitespace).
4. **Export Workflows** — Export active workflow JSONs from N8N and drop them into `~/Documents/ThurrSolutions/Thurnos/workflows/` so drift detection works on future runs.
